industry.headline: Cypress Creek Secures $3.5 Billion for Steel River Energy Center
industry.summary: Cypress Creek Energy, a U.S.-based solar and energy storage project developer, has secured $3.5 billion in financing. This funding is designated for the first two phases of the Steel River Energy Center project. The developer focuses on solar and energy storage solutions to support the project's development and financial requirements for this specific energy center initiative.

industry.headline: Military Engineer Services Issues O&M Tender for 6 MW Solar
industry.summary: Military Engineer Services (MES) has invited bids for the comprehensive operation and maintenance (O&M) of solar projects. The tender covers a 4 MW and a 2 MW solar project located at the Army area in Jodhpur, Rajasthan. These combined 6 MW of projects require professional O&M services to ensure continued operational efficiency within the military installation.

industry.headline: Bihar Regulator Approves Tariffs for 275 MW Rooftop Solar
industry.summary: Bihar Electricity Regulatory Commission (BERC) has approved tariffs discovered through competitive bidding for rooftop solar power. The procurement involves nearly 275 MW of rooftop solar power from approximately 250,000 Kutir Jyoti consumers across Bihar. This regulatory approval facilitates the procurement of solar energy from a large number of residential consumers under the specific Kutir Jyoti scheme.

tech.headline: New cooling panel uses PV and waste cooking oil
tech.summary: Researchers have designed an innovative home cooling panel system. The design incorporates PV power generation, thermoelectric cooling, and latent heat storage using waste cooking oil. Through optimized modeling, results suggest that a single panel can deliver approximately 6–15 kWh/day of cooling, providing a potential new method for residential temperature management using renewable energy.

tech.headline: Chile sets bidding terms for first energy auction including storage
tech.summary: Chile’s CNE has released preliminary terms for the 2026/01 power supply tender. This technology-neutral process favors renewables by excluding high-emission fuels and allowing storage-backed bids. Utilizing 15-year contracts and time-of-day demand blocks, the structure is designed to support photovoltaic and hybrid solar-plus-storage projects, specifically targeting daytime and evening peak coverage requirements.
tech.searchQuery: Chile releases bidding terms for first energy auct 2026

tech.headline: SNEC 2026 shows solar shifting toward system integration and AI
tech.summary: SNEC 2026 highlighted a significant industry shift from module-centric growth toward system integration. Storage and AI are becoming increasingly central to project design and value creation. At the technology level, back-contact technology gained momentum as a premium platform, while emerging applications and scenario-specific modules signaled a trend toward deeper market segmentation within the solar sector.
tech.searchQuery: SNEC 2026 wrap-up: Storage, AI and back-contact ar 2026

tech.headline: Longi develops 27.27% efficient heterojunction back-contact solar cell
tech.summary: Longi and Yangzhou University researchers developed a laser-based manufacturing strategy for HJT back-contact solar cells. This method mitigates shock-wave-induced damage, improving passivation stability and device performance. The approach resulted in a certified 27.27% efficient solar cell, providing an industrially viable pathway toward the fabrication of ultra-high-efficiency photovoltaic technology through improved laser processing techniques.

learn.title: Sodium‑Ion Batteries: Cheap Power
learn.intro: Sodium‑ion batteries promise lower material costs and safer chemistry than lithium‑ion, opening up affordable storage for renewables and electric mobility. Understanding their potential helps accelerate a more inclusive energy transition worldwide.
learn.c1_text: Sodium is 2,800 times more abundant than lithium, letting manufacturers use inexpensive feedstock. A typical 60 kWh sodium‑ion pack can cost about $400/kWh, roughly 30% cheaper than today’s lithium packs.
learn.c2_text: While energy density is modest (≈120 Wh/kg), sodium‑ion cells deliver fast charge rates (0‑80% in 15 min) and operate safely at higher temperatures, making them ideal for stationary grid storage and heavy‑duty EVs.
learn.c3_text: India’s target of 100 GW battery storage by 2030 could be met faster with sodium‑ion tech, reducing dependence on imported lithium and supporting its ambitious 450 GW renewable capacity goal.
learn.stat1: Global sodium‑ion market projected to reach $4.5 bn by 2030 (IRENA 2023)
learn.stat3: India: 30 GW battery storage under procurement (2024 data)
learn.stat4: Sodium reserves: 2,600 Mt worldwide, 1,500 Mt in India

learn.title: Offshore Wind: From Sea to Grid
learn.intro: Offshore wind farms capture stronger, steadier winds than on‑shore sites, delivering massive clean power. Mastering their design, installation, and grid integration is key to meeting global decarbonisation targets.
learn.c1_text: Turbines are spaced 7‑10 rotor diameters apart to avoid wake losses. A 12 MW offshore turbine can generate enough electricity for 8 million homes over a year, comparable to a small city.
learn.c2_text: Foundations range from monopiles (30‑50 m long) to floating platforms for deep water. Installation vessels can install up to 10 turbines per day, but weather windows limit work to 20‑30 % of the year.
learn.c3_text: India’s draft 2030 offshore wind target is 30 GW. Projects off Gujarat and Tamil Nadu are planning 3‑5 GW, leveraging the country’s 7,000 km coastline and favorable wind corridors.
learn.stat3: Cost of electricity 2023: $0.04‑0.06/kWh for large farms

learn.title: Second‑Life EV Batteries for Grid Storage
learn.intro: When electric‑vehicle batteries retire at 80 % capacity, they still hold enough energy for stationary storage. Repurposing them extends value, cuts waste, and provides low‑cost grid services.
learn.c1_text: A 70 kWh EV pack at 80 % retains ≈56 kWh, suitable for peak‑shaving. It’s like giving a used laptop a new job as a home server – still functional, just a different role.
learn.c2_text: Lifecycle cost analysis shows second‑life storage can be 30‑40 % cheaper than new batteries, delivering $0.02‑0.03/kWh for frequency regulation, making renewable integration more affordable.
learn.c3_text: Delhi’s BSES has deployed 2 MWh of repurposed Nissan Leaf packs, reducing peak demand by 5 % and earning revenue through demand‑response markets under the national ancillary services scheme.
learn.stat4: Battery reuse saves ~150 kt CO₂e per GWh stored

learn.title: Hydrogen Colours: Blue, Green & Grey
learn.intro: Hydrogen’s colour code reveals how it’s made and its carbon footprint. Knowing the differences helps policymakers and investors choose truly clean pathways for a low‑carbon economy.
learn.c1_text: Produced by electrolysis using renewable electricity. For every kilogram, emissions are < 1 kg CO₂e, comparable to a cup of coffee, making it the cleanest option.
learn.c2_text: Steam‑methane reforming paired with carbon capture (≈90 % capture rate). While cheaper than green, residual emissions are ~2 kg CO₂ per kg H₂, similar to a short domestic flight.
learn.c3_text: India’s National Hydrogen Mission aims for 5 MT of green hydrogen by 2030, with pilot blue projects in Gujarat leveraging existing natural‑gas infrastructure and CCS pilots.
learn.stat2: Blue H₂ share 2023: 30 % of total production
learn.stat4: CCS capture efficiency avg 85 % in blue plants

learn.title: How Electric Vehicles Work
learn.intro: EVs convert electricity into motion with high efficiency, offering zero‑tailpipe emissions. Grasping their core components—motors, regenerative braking, and charging—empowers consumers and planners to accelerate clean mobility.
learn.c1_text: Most EVs use permanent‑magnet synchronous motors delivering 90 % efficiency. Think of it like a high‑performance fan that spins faster with less energy than a gasoline engine’s piston.
learn.c2_text: When you decelerate, the motor acts as a generator, feeding up to 30 % of kinetic energy back into the battery—equivalent to recapturing the energy of descending a 3‑storey building.
learn.c3_text: India sold 650,000 EVs in 2023, spurred by the Faster Adoption and Manufacturing of Hybrid & EV (FAME) scheme, which subsidises up to ₹1 lakh per vehicle.
learn.stat1: Global EV stock 2023: 16 million units (US DOE)
learn.stat3: India: 45 GW solar‑plus‑storage targets to support EV charging
learn.stat4: Fast‑charge (150 kW) adds 80 % range in ~30 min

learn.title: Renewable Purchase Obligation in India
learn.intro: RPO mandates that Indian distribution companies (DISCOMs) source a set share of electricity from renewables. This policy drives market demand, spurs investment, and accelerates the nation’s clean‑energy transition.
learn.c1_text: DISCOMs must procure 30 % of their supply from renewable sources by 2030. Failure incurs penalties of up to ₹0.50/kWh, creating a strong financial incentive to buy green power.
learn.c2_text: RPOs are met through Power Purchase Agreements, renewable certificates, or direct generation. Certificates act like “green credits” that can be traded, similar to carbon credits.
learn.c3_text: Maharashtra’s RPO reached 24 % in 2023, prompting 6 GW of new solar projects and boosting local manufacturing under the Make‑in‑India scheme.
learn.stat3: Renewable capacity added via RPO 2022‑23: 12 GW
